parsed.summary: The v0.4.0→v0.4.6 cumulative diff introduces retry, error-dispatch, and job-tracking infrastructure but leaves the critical integration layer in glm-client.mjs (155 lines changed) without a dedicated test file. The three most dangerous modules—retry, error classification, and the client that wires them—are individually tested but their integration is not, creating a high-risk gap for silent retries on non-idempotent operations, misclassified errors that suppress retry, and state corruption under concurrent job dispatch.

findings.title: glm-client.mjs has 155 lines of changes but no dedicated test file
findings.body: glm-client.mjs is the integration point that wires retry.mjs and bigmodel-errors.mjs into actual API calls. It has the largest change footprint of any module (155 lines) yet has no corresponding tests/glm-client.test.mjs. The individual modules (retry: 352 test lines, bigmodel-errors: 189 test lines) have good coverage, but the integration layer where misclassified errors cause silent suppression or where retries hit non-idempotent endpoints is completely untested. A regression here would be user-visible (hung jobs, duplicate submissions, or silently dropped results) and hard to diagnose because the unit tests for each submodule would still pass.
findings.recommendation: Add tests/glm-client.test.mjs covering at minimum: (1) retry integration on transient BigModel errors, (2) error-classification fallback when bigmodel-errors.mjs returns unknown, (3) idempotency behavior when retry fires on a request that may have already been accepted server-side, (4) concurrent dispatch interleaving with tracked-jobs state.

findings.title: tracked-jobs.mjs (78 lines changed) has no test coverage
findings.body: tracked-jobs.mjs manages job lifecycle state and is modified in this diff. It interacts with retry logic and glm-client dispatch. Without tests, race conditions in job-state transitions (e.g., a job marked 'completed' while a retry is still in-flight, or a stale job resuming after a timeout) are undetectable until production failure. The module name 'tracked-jobs' implies mutable shared state, which is the highest-risk category for concurrent corruption.
findings.recommendation: Add tests/tracked-jobs.test.mjs covering: (1) concurrent start/complete transitions, (2) job resumption after simulated retry exhaustion, (3) stale-state detection and cleanup, (4) idempotency of status updates.

findings.title: process.mjs deleted but import cleanup completeness is unverifiable
findings.body: scripts/lib/process.mjs was removed (67 lines deleted, — in diff stat). The check-imports.mjs script was modified (5 lines changed), suggesting an import-rewrite effort, but no CI gate or test verifies that no remaining import of process.mjs exists in runtime code. A missed import will produce a runtime ModuleNotFoundError that only triggers when the specific code path executes—likely in production under load rather than in development.
findings.recommendation: Verify with `grep -r "from.*['\"].*lib/process['\"]" scripts/` that no remaining imports exist. Add a CI check (or extend check-imports.mjs) to assert that deleted modules are not referenced anywhere in the codebase, not just that existing imports resolve.

findings.title: Retry on potentially non-idempotent operations without request deduplication guard
findings.body: The retry.mjs module (203 lines new) provides generic retry infrastructure used by glm-client.mjs. If the underlying API calls are not idempotent (e.g., job submission, review dispatch), a retry after a network timeout could result in duplicate operations. The diff shows no deduplication key, request-id header, or idempotency token mechanism. This is the classic 'at-least-once delivery without dedup' pattern that causes data duplication under transient failures.
findings.recommendation: Either (1) document which API endpoints are idempotent and restrict retries to those, or (2) add an idempotency-key header to outgoing requests so the server can deduplicate, or (3) add a guard in glm-client.mjs that checks tracked-jobs state before retrying a submission to avoid double-dispatch.

next_steps: Add tests/glm-client.test.mjs covering retry+error-dispatch integration before shipping v0.4.7.
Add tests/tracked-jobs.test.mjs with concurrent-state and resumption scenarios.
Run `grep -rn 'process' scripts/lib/*.mjs scripts/*.mjs` to confirm no dangling imports from deleted process.mjs.
Verify idempotency semantics of any API endpoint that retry.mjs can hit—document or add guards.
